app.title: Distilllab
brand.name: Distilllab
chat.rail.title: Sessions
chat.rail.description: Keep draft and active conversation states reachable without losing the selected session.
chat.session.draftDescription: Pre-persistence workspace. The first send creates the real session.
chat.session.activeDescription: Project Delta analysis with one in-progress evidence run.
chat.session.recentBDescription: Earlier exploration for object relations and naming.
chat.draft.title: Draft Session
chat.draft.description: Use this shell surface for the next composed prompt before an active run is underway.
chat.draft.card.title: Draft Composer Placeholder
chat.draft.card.description: Later tasks can replace this region with the full timeline and composer without changing shell routing.
chat.draft.emptyDescription: Start with a task, transcript, or working question. The first message creates the session and opens a real run surface.
chat.draft.promptA.title: Distill a session
chat.draft.promptA.description: Turn a coding or research exchange into structured work items and assets.
chat.draft.promptB.title: Extract work items
chat.draft.promptB.description: Break a messy discussion into explicit tasks, constraints, and open threads.
chat.draft.promptC.title: Shape a project
chat.draft.promptC.description: Create a project frame and connect the next message to an evolving work graph.
chat.draft.promptD.title: Create assets
chat.draft.promptD.description: Promote useful notes and outputs into reusable assets after the first run.
chat.draft.composerPlaceholder: Describe the work you want to distill into structure...
chat.draft.hint: The first send transitions from draft semantics into an active session shell.
chat.active.title: Active Session
chat.active.description: The selected session stays active while the shell switches between chat, canvas, and settings surfaces.
chat.active.card.title: Timeline Placeholder
chat.active.card.description: This reserved center region will host the formal conversation timeline in later tasks.
chat.active.bannerMeta: One user turn, one assistant response, and one in-progress evidence distillation run.
chat.active.emptySelectionMeta: Select a session from the rail to inspect its timeline.
chat.active.userMessage: Analyze the latest results for Project Delta.
chat.active.assistantMessage: Starting the analysis now. I am gathering evidence, updating active work items, and checking which assets should stay linked to this run.
chat.active.progressBody: One work item updated, two related assets linked, and the current object graph is synchronized with the latest evidence.
chat.active.toolSuccessBody: Retrieved 1,240 evidence rows from the Delta analysis set. No continuity gaps were found in the imported run history.
chat.active.toolWorkingBody: Deriving the next stable work-item state from the latest anomalies and linked evidence.
chat.active.composerPlaceholder: Type a command or follow-up question...
canvas.rail.title: Canvas Scope
canvas.rail.description: Switch between global structure and a focused detail view.
canvas.scope.globalDescription: Recent Active Set stays explicit while projects, work items, and assets remain visible around it.
canvas.scope.detailDescription: Center one object, show nearby context, and keep inspection details alongside it.
shell.hero.title: Distilllab Desktop
shell.hero.description: Phase 0 keeps the existing debug controls intact while adding lightweight shell labels for navigation, draft sessions, canvas previews, and settings.
draft.title: New Session
draft.description: Start from a clean draft session before the full desktop shell lands.
run.title: Active Run
run.description: Active run rendering stays lightweight for now while the shell proves both themes across generated output surfaces.
canvas.global.title: Canvas Global View
canvas.global.description: The shell reserves a dedicated object-space surface for global structure and cross-project shape.
canvas.sidebar.title: Recent Active Set
canvas.sidebar.description: Keep the current object model explicit instead of implying it only by placement.
canvas.sidebar.recentActiveBody: Analysis, reporting, and linked assets are centered here. Older objects stay visible as surrounding context.
canvas.sidebar.modeRecentBody: Center the working set and preserve peripheral context.
canvas.sidebar.modeAllBody: Project inventory remains visible in the outline below.
canvas.sidebar.modeFiltersBody: Static filter chips preview future graph slicing.
canvas.sidebar.modeSearchBody: Jump to object anchors and local clusters.
canvas.sidebar.modeOutlineBody: Projects, work items, and assets stay readable as hierarchy.
canvas.sidebar.searchPlaceholder: Find object anchors, assets, or work items
canvas.global.recentSetBody: The center grouping is intentional: this is the working set, not just whatever happens to be in the middle.
canvas.global.projectBody: Workspace anchor for linked analysis, reporting, and review work.
canvas.global.analysisBody: Baseline metric derivation from historical repository data and current run outputs.
canvas.global.reportingBody: Draft generation of executive summaries and technical logs.
canvas.global.reviewBody: Stakeholder gate verification for phase-gate transition.
canvas.global.peripheralBody: Legacy migration archives remain visible but de-emphasized outside the active set.
canvas.detail.title: Canvas Detail View
canvas.detail.description: Focused detail view keeps room for future object inspection and scoped emphasis.
canvas.detail.focusBody: Analyzing the latest Delta results, correlating telemetry with baseline patterns, and identifying structural drift in the current sprint.
settings.card.title: Settings Surface
settings.card.description: Preferences stay in Settings so later tasks can expand this area without moving the debug tools again.
settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Appearance and language live in the non-debug settings section and rerender visible labels immediately.
settings.rail.title: Settings
settings.rail.description: General preferences stay separate from the migrated debug workspace.
settings.language.label: Language
settings.appearance.theme.description: Theme changes apply immediately and stay saved in desktop UI preferences.
settings.debug.visibility.label: Show debug panel
settings.debug.visibility.description: Keep the migrated Phase 0 debug workspace available in Settings > Debug.
settings.debug.visibility.developmentNot: Development builds keep Settings > Debug reachable even when this preference is turned off.
settings.debug.hidden.title: Debug Surface Hidden
settings.debug.hidden.description: Debug tools are currently hidden by desktop preferences, so this section stays reserved without showing the legacy controls.
debug.description: The current Phase 0 page stays available here while the future shell is still under construction.
inspector.chat.title: Chat Inspector
inspector.chat.description: Keep room for session context, pinned excerpts, and structured outputs.
inspector.chat.draft.stateBody: Draft mode. Structured objects appear here only after work is created or explicitly linked.
inspector.chat.draft.emptyBody: Objects appear after the first send creates or links them.
inspector.chat.active.goalValue: Distill the latest Delta evidence into updated work items, linked assets, and a clearer project state.
inspector.chat.active.agentBody: Focused on extracting anomalies, preserving structure, and linking outputs to related work objects.
inspector.canvas.title: Canvas Inspector
inspector.canvas.description: Keep the current canvas model readable without implying that every canvas state already has a selected object.
inspector.canvas.global.modelBody: The global canvas describes the object world and its active working set without implying a selected object.
inspector.canvas.global.scopePrimaryValu: Recent Active Set across Project Delta
inspector.canvas.global.scopeSecondaryVa: Project anchor, work items, linked assets, and peripheral context
inspector.canvas.detail.stateBody: The detail canvas centers one object while preserving its local neighborhood and nearby relation cues.
inspector.canvas.detail.statusSecondaryV: Running with linked reporting and review context
inspector.settings.title: Settings Notes
inspector.settings.description: Preferences persist across shell switches, and Debug remains nested inside Settings.
debug.panel.config.title: LLM Config Bar
debug.panel.config.description: All session and debug LLM calls read from the current provider and model configured here.
debug.panel.timeline.title: Session Timeline
debug.panel.timeline.description: Use the formal session flow below to send messages and inspect the current timeline.
debug.placeholder.providerJson: Optional raw provider JSON, compatible with opencode provider entries
debug.placeholder.attachments: Optional: one file path per line
status.createdSessionCouldNotParse: Created session, but could not parse id: {response}
status.error.enterSessionId: Error: please create or enter a session id first.
status.error.messageRequired: Error: please enter a message first.
status.error.providerModelRequired: Error: current provider and current model are required.
status.error.sessionIdMessageRequired: Error: session id and session message are required.
status.error.enterSourceId: Error: please enter a source id first.
